skill.name: internal-comms-anthropic
skill.description: Guidelines for drafting internal communications such as 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQ answers, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.
skill.summary: Draft structured internal communications from company context and source material.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high-risk crypto patterns, network URLs, reconnaissance terms, and command execution patterns. Review found these were false positives caused by ordinary prose, Apache License text, and Markdown code formatting for filenames; no executable code, prompt injection, credential handling, or data exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Workplace Data Access Requires User Authorization
medium_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to gather context from Slack, email, calendar, documents, and external press when those tools are available. This is aligned with the skill purpose, but users should only grant access to approved company sources and should review outputs for sensitive internal information before sharing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data-access guidance is explicit and repeated across examples. It is a privacy consideration, not malicious behavior, because access depends on user-granted tools and no exfiltration endpoint is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Terms in Prose
low_findings.description: The static analyzer matched substrings inside normal words such as description, desired, designated, describing, and provides. These lines do not define cryptographic algorithms, implement hashing, or process secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed each location is documentation or license prose. No code, cryptographic API, or security-sensitive operation appears at these lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown File References Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Lines in SKILL.md use backticks to format example file paths from the examples directory. They are not Ruby or shell backtick execution and contain no executable command syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown inline-code delimiters around filenames. The skill contains prose instructions only and no Ruby, shell, or script file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Apache License URLs
low_findings.description: The only hardcoded URLs are standard Apache License links. They are not fetched by code and do not create network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs occur in the unmodified Apache License text. There is no code path that sends data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms in Newsletter Guidance
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged line 7 in the newsletter example, but the line only instructs writers to use first-person company voice. It does not request host, network, account, or environment reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text says newsletter bullets may use we did phrasing. No system discovery command, target enumeration, or sensitive environment query appears there.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear internal updates, but each format requires different structure and source material. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft newsletters, 3P updates, FAQs, and general company messages.
actual_capabilities: Drafts 3P updates with Progress, Plans, and Problems sections.
Creates company-wide newsletter drafts for Slack and email.
Formats FAQ answers from employee questions and official context.
Guides general internal communications by audience, purpose, tone, and format.
Suggests source gathering from approved Slack, email, calendar, and document tools.
limitations: Does not provide live company context unless the user grants tool access or supplies source material.
Does not verify confidential or legal claims without authoritative company sources.
Does not enforce company approval workflows before distribution.
Does not include automation scripts or integrations.

use_cases.title: Weekly Team 3P Update
use_cases.description: Turn team progress, next plans, and blockers into a concise update for leadership.

use_cases.title: Company Newsletter Draft
use_cases.description: Summarize major company announcements, milestones, and leadership updates for Slack or email.

use_cases.title: Employee FAQ Summary
use_cases.description: Collect recurring employee questions and draft short answers based on official company materials.

prompt_templates.title: Create a 3P Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to write a 3P update for the product team. Progress: shipped onboarding changes and closed 12 bugs. Plans: finish admin dashboard and start beta testing. Problems: design review is delayed.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Company Newsletter
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a broad update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a company newsletter for this week. Use the notes I provide, group items into clear sections, and keep each bullet short for Slack and email.

prompt_templates.title: Answer Internal FAQs
prompt_templates.prompt: Create FAQ answers from these employee questions. Use official source notes first, flag uncertain answers, and mark questions that need executive input.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Executive Communication
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with audience and tone constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write an internal leadership update for all managers. Clarify missing audience, purpose, tone, and formatting details before drafting. Prioritize decisions, impact, and next steps.

output_examples.input: Write a weekly 3P for the infrastructure team from notes about uptime work, migration plans, and staffing blockers.
output_examples.output: The output is a short team update with one header and three labeled sections: Progress, Plans, and Problems.

output_examples.input: Create a company newsletter from a list of product launches, leadership announcements, and hiring updates.
output_examples.output: The output is a grouped newsletter with concise bullets suitable for Slack and email distribution.

output_examples.input: Summarize repeated employee questions about a new planning process and draft answers.
output_examples.output: The output is a set of question and answer pairs with links or notes pointing to authoritative sources.

best_practices: Provide source notes, audience, time period, and distribution channel before asking for a draft.
Review generated messages for confidential information before sharing them broadly.
Use official company documents for claims about strategy, policy, finance, or people changes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to invent company facts or metrics.
Do not use broad tool access without confirming the scope of sources to review.
Do not publish generated communications without human approval from the responsible owner.

faq.question: What communication formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQ answers, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, incident reports, and general internal messages.

faq.question: Does it connect to Slack, email, or calendar by itself?
faq.answer: No. It only recommends using those sources when the host AI tool already has approved access.

faq.question: Can it write a message from rough notes?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the notes, audience, time period, and desired channel for the best draft.

faq.question: Can it summarize company-wide questions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can turn recurring employee questions into concise FAQ entries when source material is provided.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential company information?
faq.answer: Use normal internal review controls. The skill has no code, but outputs may include sensitive information from provided sources.

faq.question: Does it include scripts or automation?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only and provides writing guidance, examples, and formatting rules.
